name: CMN Substrate
synopsis: Shared Rust library for CMN — data models, cryptographic verification, tree hashing, URI parsing, and JSON schema validation used by both Hypha and Synapse
intent: Provide the common foundation shared between Hypha (client) and Synapse (indexer): spore/mycelium data models, Ed25519 signature verification, BLAKE3 content hashing, CMN URI parsing, and spore.core.json schema validation
mutations: Rust implementation of the CMN protocol core — Ed25519 signature verification, BLAKE3 content hashing, JCS canonicalization, and content-addressed URI construction for all protocol artifacts (spore, mycelium, taste, cmn.json)

Zero-I/O data model layer — strongly typed Rust structs for Spore, Mycelium, Taste, CmnEntry with two-layer signature verification (core + capsule), bond relations (spawned_from, absorbed_from, depends_on, follows, extends), and deterministic JSON formatting
WASM-compatible tree hashing (blob_tree_blake3_nfc) — Git-like Merkle tree with Unicode NFC normalization, configurable exclusion rules, and total size computation
Embedded JSON Schema validation for all five protocol schemas (cmn.json, mycelium.json, spore.json, spore-core.json, taste.json) with offline validation and schema type detection
Optional async HTTP client for fetching manifests with SSRF protection (rejects private IPs, localhost), Synapse API integration (search, lineage, pulse), and bearer token auth
Optional tar+zstd archive extraction with security hardening — rejects symlinks, path traversal, absolute paths, enforces byte and file count limits, distinguishes malicious vs failed errors

bonds.reason: Implements core CMN data structures and verification logic per the specification
